ref: The Lord said, ‚ÄúGo get Isaac, your only son, the one you dearly love! Take him to the land of Moriah, and I will show you a mountain where you must sacrifice him to me on the fires of an altar.‚Äù
text: ∆Üzele ye k…õ, ‚ÄúFa …õ ra nrenya ne, …õ ra kokye Ayezeke, m…î…î la w…î ahonle nwo la, na k…î Molaya az…õl…õ zo k…îyela ye b…î azu w…î awoka ne m…î ko m…î…î mebahile w…î la azo maa me.‚Äù
translated: And he said, ‚ÄúTake your son, your only son Isaac, whom you love, and go to the region of Moriah, and sacrifice him there as a burnt offering on one of the mountains that I will show you.‚Äù

ref: ‚ÄúDon't get in our way,‚Äù the crowd answered. ‚ÄúYou're a foreigner. What right do you have to order us around? We'll do worse things to you than we're going to do to them.‚Äù The crowd kept arguing with Lot. Finally, they rushed toward the door to break it down.
text: Na b…õzele ye k…õ, ‚ÄúDwazo …õk…õ ne! W…îm…î …õdaw…î mumua ne …õle …õy…õvol…õ w…î suakpole ye azo. W…îm…î …õbahakyi y…õ k…îme nu nd…õnebuavol…õ. Y…õ nee w…î bali ye …õseseb…õ b…îb…î y…õad…õla menli …õhye m…î.‚Äù Akee b…õholole L…î…îto anwo zo na b…õy…õle k…õ b…õkabu anlenk…õ ne.
translated: And they replied, ‚ÄúIt is true! We saw him at the river bank where we went to fetch water. We saw him turn into a crocodile and disappear into the water. We are telling you the truth, and if you don‚Äôt believe us, go and see for yourself.‚Äù Then they pointed to the direction of the river and told them to go and search for him.

ref: Deep water was still everywhere, and when the dove could not find a place to land, it flew back to the boat. Then Noah held out his hand and helped it back in.

translated: When the moon and the sun are shining, the stars hide and rest. The moon has its own time and its own way, and God created day and night so that each has its own time and its own way.

ref: ‚ÄúYou, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law may now leave the boat.
text: ‚Äú∆ê nee …õ ye nee …õ mra nee b…õ mraal…õ ne m…î b…õvi …õl…õne ne anu b…õdwu aze.
translated: It is not good for a child to be stubborn and disobedient to the elders and parents.

ref: Abraham went quickly to his tent and said to Sarah, ‚ÄúHurry! Get a large sack of flour and make some bread.‚Äù
text: Ebileham ziale wolole ye ndanl…õ sua ne anu nd…õnd…õ na …îh…îzele S…õla k…õ, ‚ÄúKa …õ nwo suzu esamo kpal…õ ekyi y…õ kpanwo.‚Äù
translated: The old woman then said to the child, ‚ÄúGo and throw the stone into the river and let us go.‚Äù
